lit: But nor was he a lamb.
sm: But neither was he a lamb.
emd: Editorial brackets preserved in the source form [ἀλ]λ᾽; analysis is lexicalized as ἀλλ᾽.

syn.mc: Adversative main clause continuing the corrective contrast begun in line 29.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the line completes the concessive setup of line 30 by negating the image.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Verb: ἦν. Predicate nominative: ἀμνός.
syn.rhet: Second corrective denial after line 29: not a sheep, not even a lamb.

lit: For the type, on the one hand, came to be,
sm: For the type did indeed come into being,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; γάρ explains the prior denials by distinguishing type from reality.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τύπος. Verb: ἐγένετο. No direct object.
syn.rhet: μὲν sets up the antithesis with line 33: type versus truth.

lit: [But] the truth was being found.
sm: But the reality was being disclosed.
emd: Editorial brackets preserved; analysis lexicalizes the clause as ἡ δὲ ἀλήθεια ηὑρίσκετο.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ἀλήθεια. Verb: ηὑρίσκετο. No direct object.
syn.rhet: Type/reality antithesis is stated with compressed symmetry.

lit: For instead of the lamb a son came to be
sm: For in place of the lamb there appeared a Son,
emd: Editorial brackets preserved within [ἀμνοῦ υἱὸ]ς; analysis follows the restored lexical sequence ἀμνοῦ υἱὸς.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἀντὶ + genitive expresses substitution.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: the christological reality. Verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative/result: υἱός. Prepositional phrase: ἀντὶ τοῦ ἀμνοῦ.
syn.rhet: Substitutional parallelism begins: lamb -> Son.

lit: And instead of the sheep, a man,
sm: and in place of the sheep, a man,
emd: Editorial brackets preserved in ἄν[θρωπ]ος; verb ἐγένετο is implied from line 34.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the verb is understood from line 34.
syn.svo: Implied subject/verb: [the reality] ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: ἄνθρωπος. Prepositional phrase: ἀντὶ τοῦ προβάτου.
syn.rhet: Second member of the substitutional sequence: sheep -> man.

src: ἐν δὲ τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ Χριστὸς ὃς κεχώρηκεν [τὰ] πάντα.
lit: And in the man, Christ, who has encompassed all things.
sm: and within the man is Christ, the one who encompasses all things.

syn.mc: Primary nominal clause with an attached relative clause.
syn.sub: Relative clause: ὃς κεχώρηκεν [τὰ] πάντα modifies Χριστός.
syn.svo: Main clause: implied copula with Χριστὸς as subject/predicate in relation to ἐν τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ. Relative clause subject: ὃς. Verb: κεχώρηκεν. Object: [τὰ] πάντα.
syn.app: Χριστός stands in close explanatory relation to the ανθρωπος-clause, expressing the climax of the substitutions.
syn.rhet: Third step of ascent: lamb -> Son, sheep -> man, in the man -> Christ who contains all things.

lit: The slaughter, then, of the sheep
sm: So then, the slaughter of the sheep

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the finite verb is delayed to line 39.
syn.svo: Subject nucleus: ἡ ... σφαγή. Dependent genitive: τοῦ προβάτου.
syn.rhet: Begins a three-member subject chain listing sacrificial elements that converge in Christ.

lit: And the application of the blood
sm: and the application of the blood

syn.mc: Coordinate subject phrase continued from line 37.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the finite verb remains delayed to line 39.
syn.svo: Coordinate subject member: ἡ ... πομπή with dependent genitive τοῦ αἵματος.
syn.rhet: Second item in the sacrificial triad.

src: καὶ ἡ τοῦ νόμου γραφὴ εἰς Χριστὸν ᾿Ιησοῦν κεχώρηκεν,
lit: And the writing of the law has converged into Christ Jesus,
sm: and the scriptural writing of the law has come to its goal in Christ Jesus,

syn.mc: Main clause completing the subject chain of lines 37-39.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause in this line; a relative clause follows in line 40.
syn.svo: Compound subject: ἡ ... σφαγὴ, καὶ ἡ ... πομπή, καὶ ἡ ... γραφή. Verb: κεχώρηκεν. Goal phrase: εἰς Χριστὸν ᾿Ιησοῦν.
syn.rhet: The triad culminates teleologically in Christ Jesus.

src: δι’ ὃν τὰ πάντα ἐν τῷ πρεσβυτέρῳ νόμῳ ἐγένετο,
lit: Through whom all things in the older law came to be,
sm: through whom all things came into being in the older law,

syn.sub: The whole line is a relative clause introduced by δι’ ὃν.
syn.svo: Subject: τὰ πάντα (neuter plural with singular verb). Verb: ἐγένετο. Prepositional phrase: ἐν τῷ πρεσβυτέρῳ νόμῳ. Relative phrase: δι’ ὃν.
syn.rhet: The clause universalizes the scope of Christ's prefiguration within the older law.

src: μᾶλλον δὲ ἐν τῷ νέῳ λόγῳ.
lit: But rather in the new word.
sm: or rather, in the new Word/discourse.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the verb ἐγένετο is understood from line 40.
syn.svo: Implied continuation: τὰ πάντα ... ἐγένετο, μᾶλλον δὲ ἐν τῷ νέῳ λόγῳ.
syn.rhet: μᾶλλον δέ corrects and heightens the older-law statement by pointing to the new λόγος.

src: καὶ γὰρ ὁ νόμος λόγος ἐγένετο,
lit: For the law became word,
sm: For the law became word/discourse,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; γάρ grounds the preceding μᾶλλον δέ.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ νόμος. Verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: λόγος.
syn.rhet: First of a rapid series of transformation clauses.

lit: And the old [became] new,
sm: and the old became new,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ὃ -> ὁ (article, not relative pronoun). The noun is elliptic and the adjective is used substantivally.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐγένετο is understood from the previous line.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ παλαιός (substantival adjective). Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate adjective: καινός.
syn.rhet: Compressed paradox of old transformed into new.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Having gone forth together from Zion and Jerusalem,
sm: having come forth from Zion and Jerusalem,

syn.mc: Circumstantial participial phrase attached to the transformation sequence of lines 42-49.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle most naturally modifies the transformed λόγος/new reality.
syn.svo: Participle: συνεξελθών. Source phrase: ἐκ Σιὼν καὶ Ἰερουσαλήμ.
syn.ptc: συνεξελθών is circumstantial, probably referring to the λόγος/new dispensation that issues from Zion and Jerusalem.
syn.rhet: Echoes prophetic diction about divine teaching going forth from Zion.

lit: And the commandment [became] grace,
sm: and the commandment became grace,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐγένετο remains understood from line 42.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ἐντολή. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: χάρις.
syn.rhet: Continues the transformation chain from old covenant terms to fulfilled realities.

lit: And the type [became] truth,
sm: and the type became reality,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the implied verb is again ἐγένετο.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τύπος. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: ἀλήθεια.
syn.rhet: Restates in brief form the line 32-33 contrast between type and truth.

lit: And the lamb [became] son,
sm: and the lamb became Son,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐγένετο remains implied.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἀμνός. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: υἱός.
syn.rhet: Pushes typology toward christological identity: lamb -> Son.

lit: And the sheep [became] man,
sm: and the sheep became man,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the verb is still implied from line 42.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ πρόβατον. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: ἄνθρωπος.
syn.rhet: Second christological ascent after lamb -> Son: sheep -> man.

lit: And the man [became] God.
sm: and the man became God.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ὃ -> ὁ (article, not relative pronoun).

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause climaxing the sequence.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; ἐγένετο is understood from line 42.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄνθρωπος. Implied verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: θεός.
syn.rhet: Climactic final ascent: man -> God.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: For, having been born as a son,
sm: For, born as a Son,

syn.mc: Participial phrase beginning a new descriptive sequence whose finite verb is delayed to a later line.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; ὡς marks the mode or aspect under which the subject is described.
syn.svo: Implicit subject: Christ. Participle: τεχθείς. Predicative complement under ὡς: υἱός.
syn.ptc: τεχθείς is circumstantial, launching the chain of parallel participles that culminates later in the resurrection statement.
syn.rhet: Begins a tightly parallel participial crescendo: as Son, as lamb, as sheep, as man.
